text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. When you refactor, you make the code cleaner and easier to understand, but the program still works exactly as before. This is important because code often becomes messy over time as new features are added. This mess is called technical debt, and if you ignore it, developers will find it harder and harder to work with the code. By refactoring, you pay off this debt by improving readability and organization. This makes future changes faster and less risky, so the software stays maintainable in the long run.
